title: Will Neymar play at World Cup 2026? Brazil star's injury latest before first game vs. Morocco - Yahoo Sports
original_text:
Will Neymar play at World Cup 2026? Brazil star's injury latest before first game vs. Morocco
Will Neymar play at World Cup 2026? Brazil star's injury latest before first game vs. Morocco originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.
Brazil will continue their proud record as the only nation to compete at every edition of the FIFA World Cup when the 2026 tournament in North America comes around.
Advertisement
The five-time champions are playing under veteran Italian coach and five-time UEFA Champions League winner Carlo Ancelotti as they plot a record-extending sixth world title.
However, there is a notable lack of optimism surrounding Brazil's prospects.
Since their most recent World Cup triumph in 2002, the Selecao have lost four out of five quarterfinals. The only time they got beyond that stage, on home soil in 2014, it ended in grand humiliation as Germany hammered them 7-1 in Belo Horizonte.
Brazil's defining star of this underwhelming period — some might say fittingly — is the superstar forward Neymar.
Advertisement
2026 WORLD CUP HQ:World Cup news | Full World Cup schedule | Buy World Cup tickets
Neymar was selected in Brazil's final roster for the World Cup. Ancelotti favoured the Santos forward over more in-form players such as Chelsea's Joao Pedro.
MORE:Inside Brazil's World Cup roster as Ancelotti faces defensive headache
Before announcing the squad in May, there was plenty of doubt over whether Ancelotti would include Neymar. A final audition for a place went badly awry the weekend prior to the squad being named. Santos went down to a 3-0 defeat against Cortiba and Neymar was mistakenly substituted when he received treatment for a knock at the side of the pitch.
Advertisement
Robinho Jr. was sent on in his place due to a mistake from the fourth official. Neymar was cautioned by the referee over his protests and attempts to re-enter the pitch.
FULL STORY:Neymar has all-time freak out after officials mistakenly call for star's substitution
Neymar is Brazil's all-time leading scorer, having gone past the great Pele with a brace against Bolivia in September 2023. A month later, in a World Cup qualifier versus Uruguay, he suffered a torn ACL and meniscus in his left knee.
The 33-year-old former Paris Saint-Germain star has not played for his country since and was overlooked for recent squads by Ancelotti, but the Italian never ruled out a return.
Advertisement
"He has to be 100%," Ancelotti told Esporte Record when asked about Neymar's place in his World Cup plans.
"There are many players who are very good, I need to choose players who are 100%. It's not just Neymar; it could be Vinicius. If Vinicius is at 90%, I'll call up another player who is at 100%, because it's a team that has a very high level of competence, especially up front. Up front, we have really many good players."
Real Madrid forward Rodrygo suffering an ACL injury in early March robbed Ancelotti of one of his first-choice attackers and opened up space for a Neymar return.
However, an injury problem of his own could yet scupper the veteran's involvement in Brazil's first game of the tournament.
Advertisement
MORE:Neymar's record at the FIFA World Cup
Is Neymar injured?
Neymar was called up for the World Cup despite struggling with a calf injury, which kept him out of both of Brazil's warm-up games against Panama and Egypt.
The country's soccer confederation said on June 8 that the forward was making promising progress as he bids to be fit for the opener against Morocco on June 13.
After medical checks on the problem, the CBF said: "The exam showed good progress in his treatment, within expected parameters.
"He will continue the recovery and physical preparation process planned by the Brazilian National Team's medical staff."
Advertisement
A report by Globo on June 9 said that, after further assessment, Neymar was targeting Brazil's second match against Haiti in Philadelphia on June 20 for his return.
MORE:A list of the most notable star players struggling with injuries ahead of the 2026 World Cup
What team does Neymar play for?
Neymar plays for Brazilian team Santos, his boyhood club which he rejoined in January 2025 following an injury-ruined stint with Saudi Pro League club Al Hilal.
On November 28, 2025, Neymar started and scored in Santos' 3-0 win over Sport — a result that moved his club out of the Campeonato Brasileirao Serie A relegation zone on goal difference with two games of the 2025 season remaining. They ended the campaign in 12th place in the standings.
Advertisement
When was Neymar's last World Cup?
Neymar played for Brazil at the Qatar 2022 World Cup, where he scored in both their knockout round matches against South Korea and Croatia.
Despite scoring a sensational solo goal in the game against Croatia, Neymar was left crestfallen as the Selecao suffered a penalty shootout defeat.
Overall, Neymar has scored eight goals in 13 appearances across three separate World Cups.
